title: Adoption trial + Merkle DAG compare: zilliztech/claude-context
description:
Two threads on zilliztech/claude-context (8.4k stars, TypeScript MCP server for semantic code search, uses Merkle DAG for incremental indexing):

Thread A — Adoption trial (what-to-steal #2):
1. Install via `claude mcp add claude-context -- npx @zilliz/claude-context-mcp@latest` with Zilliz Cloud + OpenAI embeddings
2. Index 1-2 real codebases (candidates: core-bl for lmwtfy work, or public360-mcp-server itself)
3. Use for a week across actual tasks
4. Note: retrieval quality, latency, cost (Zilliz + OpenAI embeddings), failure modes
5. Decide: keep / adopt / drop

Thread B — Merkle DAG reference reading for code-to-arch-mcp:
1. Read their Merkle DAG indexing implementation (pnpm-workspace monorepo, packages/)
2. Compare against current incremental scan state in internal/infra/persist.go
3. Document differences in approach (hashing strategy, subtree detection, change propagation)
4. Decide whether to port any patterns; commit #294 today removed unreachable modified-detection, so the implementation is actively being sharpened

Source: https://github.com/zilliztech/claude-context
Sibling: https://github.com/zilliztech/memsearch (persistent memory MCP plugin)
Background from 23-04-2026 sift: [[Building Agents That Reach Production Systems With MCP - Anthropic]]
close_reason: Thread B (Merkle DAG comparison doc) shipped in PR #4 / c6f467e. Thread A (week-long adoption trial) split out as a separate bead.

title: Add HTML output format (Option D: Mermaid-embed self-contained)
description:
Add HTML output format to `arch_generate` for human-facing codebase review. Produces a self-contained HTML file with embedded Mermaid diagram.

Scope (Option D from the CodeFlow evaluation):
- ~100 lines of new Go code
- ~600KB output file (self-contained via `go:embed`)
- Reuses the existing Mermaid renderer; no new layout logic to maintain
- Candidate API: `arch_generate --format html` or a new `arch_generate_html` tool

Defaults:
- View level: `component` (the `container` level returns empty output for Go MCP servers because they have packages and endpoints, not service-type nodes; document this)

Why Option D (Mermaid-embed) and not Option C (D3 force graph):
- Mermaid is sufficient for repos up to ~50 nodes; reference repos tested (miro-mcp-server, mediawiki-mcp-server, code-to-arch-mcp) are 15-17 nodes
- D3 path is 4-5 days vs 1 day for Mermaid-embed
- Can upgrade to D3 later if users need it for larger repos

Acceptance:
- `arch_generate` supports `format=html`
- Output file is self-contained (no network dependencies when opened in browser)
- Default view level is `component`
- Tool description documents the Go-MCP-servers-container-is-empty behavior
- Tests cover render output structure

Reference: `/tmp/codeflow-vs-code-to-arch-eval-2026-04-22.md`
Parent bead: code-to-arch-mcp-97x
close_reason: HTML output format (Option D, Mermaid-embed) shipped in PR #5 / 480716b. Self-contained ~900 KB output, defaults to component view for Go MCP servers.

title: Evaluate CodeFlow vs code-to-arch-mcp on 3 reference repos; decide on HTML-output format feature
description:
Paste: https://github.com/braedonsaunders/codeflow

Plan:
1. Run CodeFlow (hosted at codeflow-five.vercel.app) on 3 repos: miro-mcp-server, public360-mcp-server, novuhq/novu.
2. Run code-to-arch-mcp (arch_generate with Mermaid, PlantUML, C4) on same 3 repos.
3. Compare: what does each surface that the other misses?
4. Decide: should code-to-arch-mcp add an HTML-output format (self-contained D3 viewer) as a tool? Candidate API: arch_generate_html or --format html.
5. If yes: scope the feature as a follow-up bead.

Context:
- CodeFlow = single index.html, D3-based, browser-only, privacy-first
- code-to-arch-mcp = Go MCP server, agent-facing output
- Different consumers: humans vs agents. HTML output would serve humans (PR reviewers, stakeholders)
- Reference sift: AI-Knowledge/Sift Reading Log.md entry 21-04-2026

Estimated: 2-3 hours.
close_reason: Decision baked into shipped child 97x.1 (Option D Mermaid-embed). Eval purpose served; HTML format is now in main.

title: Extend arch_scan to markdown substrate (Obsidian wiki-links + relative md links)
description:
Split from 0nz. The substrate-agnostic half. Currently arch_scan parses Go imports; extend to parse markdown files and emit nodes/edges from link relationships.

Tool extension scope:
- Detect substrate by file extension at scan time: .go (existing), .md/.markdown (new)
- Markdown analyzer in internal/analyzer/markdown/ (mirror golang/, typescript/, python/ pattern)
- Parse two link types per file:
  1. Wiki-links: `[[note]]` or `[[note|display]]` (Obsidian convention)
  2. Relative markdown links: `[text](./relative.md)` and `[text](folder/note.md)`
- Output: same Node/Edge shape as Go scan (NodePackage analog: NodeNote? or treat each file as NodePackage)
- Skip code blocks (don't parse links inside fenced code)

Strategic question worth deciding before coding:
- Add NodeNote type, or reuse NodePackage with substrate metadata?
- Single arch_scan that auto-detects, or new arch_scan_markdown tool?

Acceptance:
1. Markdown analyzer with wiki-link + relative-link edge detection
2. Integration test against own AI-Knowledge directory (~150 notes; expect to find orphans + hubs)
3. Tool description updated per existing definitions.go convention
4. golangci-lint clean

Parent: code-to-arch-mcp-0nz (split for scope)
Architectural read: substrate-detection layer + graph algorithm layer (codeflow's approach)
close_reason: Implemented in feat/arch-scan-markdown. Markdown analyzer with NodeNote type, wiki-link + relative .md link extraction, code-block stripping, wikilink resolver in ResolvedEdges. Integration test on AI-Knowledge: 292 notes, 1282 raw / 905 resolved edges (71% rate), 63 hubs, 40 orphans, 44ms scan.

title: Add arch_blast_radius tool: transitive dependency closure for Go substrate
description:
Split from 0nz. The 'what depends on X?' half — complements the existing arch_dependencies (which answers 'what does X depend on?').

Tool spec:
- Name: arch_blast_radius
- Method: ArchBlastRadius
- Category: analysis
- Args: target file or package path (`target string`), plus existing Path/Repo/ScanControl pattern
- Returns: list of nodes whose transitive dependency closure includes target, with the dependency chain that reaches it
- Use case: 'if I change file X, what else needs review?'

Implementation notes:
- Reuse existing graph in ArchGraph.ResolvedEdges() to walk reverse edges
- Add reverse-edge index helper if needed (check internal/model first)
- Default depth cap to prevent runaway on circular dependencies (suggest 50; existing arch_dataflow uses 20)
- Tool description per existing 'USE WHEN / FAILS WHEN' pattern in tools/definitions.go

Acceptance:
1. arch_blast_radius implemented for Go substrate
2. ToolSpec quality reviewed (USE WHEN / FAILS WHEN format)
3. golangci-lint clean, gosec/govulncheck via CI
4. Test coverage for: simple transitive closure, depth cap, missing target node, circular deps

Parent: code-to-arch-mcp-0nz (split for scope)
Reference: braedonsaunders/codeflow design demonstrates substrate-agnostic graph algorithm
close_reason: arch_blast_radius shipped in PR #6 / 291cd52. Pure BFS algorithm in detector/blast_radius.go, handler in tools/handlers.go, 7 test cases including cycle safety and depth cap. Tool count 17 → 18.

title: Adoption trial: zilliztech/claude-context as MCP-installed semantic search
description:
Spun off from ycx after Thread B (Merkle DAG comparison doc) shipped in PR #4 / commit c6f467e.

Thread A from original ycx, kept intact:

1. Install via `claude mcp add claude-context -- npx @zilliz/claude-context-mcp@latest` with Zilliz Cloud + OpenAI embeddings
2. Index 1-2 real codebases (candidates: core-bl for lmwtfy work, or public360-mcp-server itself)
3. Use for a week across actual tasks
4. Note: retrieval quality, latency, cost (Zilliz + OpenAI embeddings), failure modes
5. Decide: keep / adopt / drop

Source: https://github.com/zilliztech/claude-context
Background: 23-04-2026 sift entry on Anthropic 'Building Agents That Reach Production Systems With MCP'.

Note: this is a multi-week trial, not a single-session task. Keep open as long as the trial is in progress.
close_reason: Won't-do for now. Multi-week adoption trial cost (Zilliz Cloud + OpenAI embeddings spend, indexing 1-2 real codebases, week of actual use) is high relative to the value, given the portfolio is already dense. Re-open if priorities shift toward semantic code search as a daily tool. Original Thread A spec preserved in the bead description for future reference.

title: Add arch_blast_radius tool + extend arch_scan to markdown substrate
description: Two related tool additions inspired by braedonsaunders/codeflow (https://github.com/braedonsaunders/codeflow, 2.5K stars, last push 26-04-2026) sift'd 26-04-2026. **Tool 1: arch_blast_radius**. Takes a file path and returns the transitive dependency closure (what depends on X). Complements existing arch_dependencies (what X depends on). Use case: Claude Code asks 'if I change file X what else needs review?' Currently no tool answers this directly. **Tool 2: extend arch_scan to markdown substrate**. Currently arch_scan parses Go imports. Extend to handle Obsidian vault / markdown directory as input. Edges: wiki-links `[[note]]` and relative markdown links `[text](./relative.md)`. Output: same node/edge shape as Go scan. Use case: 'scan my AI-Knowledge directory and tell me which notes are orphans / hubs / highly-cross-linked.' Already partially covered by knowledge-lint skill but knowledge-lint doesn't produce a graph artifact. **Strategic move**: codeflow demonstrates substrate-agnostic graph algorithm (one tool, multiple substrates: code + obsidian + future). Worth considering as v2 architecture for code-to-arch-mcp: substrate-detection layer + graph algorithm layer. Reading log: 26-04-2026 Great-rated entry. **Acceptance**: (1) arch_blast_radius implemented for Go substrate, (2) arch_scan extended to .md substrate with wiki-link edge detection, (3) integration test against own AI-Knowledge directory (~150 notes, expect to find orphans/hubs), (4) tool descriptions updated per existing definitions.go conventions, (5) golangci-lint clean, gosec clean, govulncheck clean, (6) ToolSpec quality reviewed by go-mcp-reviewer agent.
close_reason: Split into two child beads: arch_blast_radius (transitive dep closure) and markdown substrate extension. Both retain the original acceptance criteria and reference. Parent purpose (group both ideas) achieved through children.

title: Add clean-clone CI job to catch WIP-dependent main commits
description:
Add a CI job that builds and tests from a fresh clone (not a cached workspace).

Discovered during 22-04-2026 SDK bump sweep: main HEAD was found to not compile standalone. Commit `e346a99` added calls to `safepath.ValidateOutputPath` in `tools/handlers.go:631, 882` but the function definition only existed in uncommitted local WIP. The bug went undetected because every local checkout had the WIP.

Fixed retroactively in commit `b8fbdf9` (safepath: add ValidateOutputPath for not-yet-existing file paths).

Fix to prevent recurrence:
1. Add a `clean-clone` job to CI that does `git clone`, `go build ./...`, `go test ./...` from a pristine checkout on every push
2. Ensure this job runs parallel to (not cached alongside) other CI jobs
3. Job must fail if main cannot stand on its own without local modifications

This is a belt-and-suspenders for solo-maintained repos where every dev workspace tends to have local WIP.
close_reason: Clean-clone CI job merged in PR #3 (squash 92cd3d8)
